title: Mounting over a symlink mounts over its target
body:
### Issue Description

Using `-v` to mount a volume onto a path that happens to be a symlink results in mounting it over the _destination_ of that symlink.

Either this behavior should not be the default (my preference), or there should be an option to `-v` corresponding to `O_NOFOLLOW` or `RESOLVE_NO_SYMLINKS`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```console
mkdir foo bar
ln -s does-not-exist foo/dst
echo test > bar/data
podman run --rm -it -v "$(realpath foo):/foo" -v "$(realpath bar):/foo/dst" alpine:3.21 cat /foo/dst/data
```

### Describe the results you received

The `podman` command exits with:

```
Error: crun: creating `/foo/dst`: openat2 `foo/dst`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

### Describe the results you expected

The `bar` directory should be mounted over the path `/foo/dst`, so the `cat` command should succeed and print `test`.

### podman info output

comments.body:
Mounting is performed by the OCI runtime and not podman so unless the runtimes already expose such an option it must be implemented there first. AFAIK mounting on top of a symlink is only possible with AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW
cc @giuseppe 

In any case this is not a bug so I mark it as feature
yes this must be handled by the OCI runtime and AFAIK that is not currently supported in the OCI runtime specs, so we should first try to get it there before implementing it as a crun-only feature.

> mkdir foo bar
> ln -s does-not-exist foo/dst
> echo test > bar/data
> podman run --rm -it -v "$(realpath foo):/foo" -v "$(realpath bar):/foo/dst" alpine:3.21 cat /foo/dst/data

I don't think this can work though because you can't bind mount a directory on top of a symlink, you can mount only a file
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> > mkdir foo bar
> > ln -s does-not-exist foo/dst
> > echo test > bar/data
> > podman run --rm -it -v "$(realpath foo):/foo" -v "$(realpath bar):/foo/dst" alpine:3.21 cat /foo/dst/data
> 
> I don't think this can work though because you can't bind mount a directory on top of a symlink, you can mount only a file

@giuseppe: I may be missing something, but this *does* work in Docker, so clearly this is possible, somehow.

Anyway, now that your PR has been merged in crun, is there any chance of getting a fix into podman?
